title: Add test/apiv2/rest_api tests to make target
body:
* renamed old API tests to not be discovered, they do not pass
* Updated the API tests to use a pristine storage configuration
* Skipped attach test, it needs to be re-written
* Code re-formatter with `black` formatter

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: @jwhonce looks like at least some of the tests are kicking up their heals.
@cevich I suspect the errors are caused by python version mismatch. Which version of python should I have targeted? I'm on fc33 so the code currently runs on python 3.9

> @jwhonce looks like at least some of the tests are kicking up their heals.

@TomSweeneyRedHat yeah, I suspect a change in the python libraries. I need to get with @cevich Monday.

> Which version of python should I have targeted? I'm on fc33 so the code currently runs on python 3.9

My recommendation is to shoot low, not high.  There's no rhyme/reason across distros, and Ubuntu/debian tends to lag begind, esp. the LTS and/or "Prior Ubuntu" flavor (whichever is older).  IIRC, 3.7 is a "safe place" for the next year or so (but worth checking).
